title: Implement action-item generation, stable task identity, and review-lane lifecycle
description:
## Background
The source plan's Phase 3 is one canonical workstream: turn raw communication into candidate work, classify uncertainty visibly, and maintain stable task identity over time. Distillation, the `needs_review` lane, selective LLM escalation, fingerprints, evidence linking, and task lifecycle semantics are tightly coupled. Splitting them encouraged repeated restatement of confidence bands, provenance, promotion recommendations, review-lane behavior, and replay stability.

The stronger cut is a single canonical Phase 3 bead that owns action-item generation together with stable task identity and lifecycle management. This keeps the review lane, task fingerprints, and selective LLM use in one coherent contract instead of scattering them across adjacent beads.

## Technical Approach
1. Implement configurable task-like phrasing and contextual signals, including English, Chinese, and mixed-language cues.
2. Run deterministic heuristics first and emit class, confidence, reason code, provenance, and promotion recommendation.
3. Route only uncertain or long-context cases to the active LLM provider.
4. Support heuristics-only operation when provider access or budgets are unavailable.
5. Define the fingerprint and task ID policy using normalized ask text, source anchor, assignee cues, and bounded time windows.
6. Implement conservative merge rules so repeated evidence usually links before aggressive cross-source merging occurs.
7. Maintain task evidence records that accumulate excerpts, links, and confidence deltas.
8. Preserve manual override state across replay unless intentionally reset.
9. Expose promotion recommendations, stable task IDs, and `needs_review` metadata for rendering and feedback workflows.
10. Define a minimal review-lane export contract for candidate tasks so later rendering and plannotator feedback work build on a stable interface instead of inventing their own task-review semantics.

## Success Criteria
- The system can classify into context, follow-up, task, and needs-review.
- Multilingual task-like signals are configurable rather than hard-coded as final truth.
- LLM use is selective, explainable, and optional.
- Stable task IDs survive replay on the fixture corpus.
- Repeated asks update existing tasks when the fingerprint match is strong.
- Lower-confidence items remain in a review lane instead of silently disappearing or being auto-promoted as durable open work.
- Manual task completion and other overrides persist across reruns.

## Test Plan
- Unit-test heuristics against multilingual fixture cases and known false-positive/false-negative examples.
- Add routing tests for escalation thresholds and heuristics-only fallback behavior.
- Add golden tests for emitted reason codes, confidence bands, and promotion recommendations.
- Add replay tests proving task IDs remain stable across reruns.
- Add merge/evidence-linking tests for repeated asks, near-duplicates, and cross-source ambiguity.
- Add lifecycle tests for snooze, dismiss, merge, supersede, and completion semantics.
- Add contract tests for the minimal review-lane export shape consumed by rendering and feedback flows.

## Considerations
- Recall-first does not mean unbounded noise; expose ambiguity so review can improve quality over time.
- Start conservative on cross-source merging; false merges are harder to recover from than extra evidence links.
- Keep provenance and explanation fields close to the task registry so review stays intelligible.
- Keep the minimal review export intentionally small; the later feedback workflow bead should harden the user-facing artifact, not redefine task identity.

title: Implement vault-safe note rendering and promotion flow
description:
## Background
The vault is not a generic output directory. AGENTS.md defines a maintenance contract: new source material goes to `raw/` first, distilled action items land in the relevant daily note first, still-open work is promoted into `area/current tasks/index.md`, and user-authored content outside machine-owned regions must not be overwritten. This bead operationalizes that contract.

The plan also requires stable per-item block IDs inside explicit managed sections so rerenders remain bounded and idempotent. This is the trust boundary for note automation.

## Technical Approach
1. Implement raw-note or raw-context rendering into `raw/` with provenance and backlinks.
2. Render distilled work into the relevant `daily/YYYY-MM-DD.md` note first.
3. Promote durable open work into `area/current tasks/index.md` while keeping lower-confidence items in a review lane.
4. Use explicit machine-owned section markers and stable block IDs for item-level replacement.
5. Preserve user-authored content, prefer updating canonical notes over creating near-duplicates, and defer broad people/project/area automation until the core surfaces are stable.

## Success Criteria
- Raw, daily, and Current Tasks outputs follow the vault contract.
- Rerendering updates only the intended machine-owned blocks.
- Open tasks retain backlinks to source context and daily-note entries.
- Duplicate bullets are avoided through stable task IDs or strong fingerprints.

## Test Plan
- Add golden-file tests for raw-note, daily-note, and Current Tasks rendering.
- Add block-safety tests proving nearby user-authored text is unchanged after rerender.
- Add replay tests showing stable, idempotent updates under repeated runs.

## Considerations
- Safety matters more than breadth: keep people/project/area auto-updates manual or opt-in until these core surfaces are stable.

title: Implement structured feedback and plannotator review flow
description:
## Background
Recall-first systems improve only if the human reviewer can cheaply confirm useful work, dismiss noise, mark wrong classes, point out missed tasks, merge duplicates, or snooze items. The plan chooses plannotator plus Markdown artifacts as the initial review surface, with machine-parseable feedback stored in a YAML sidecar by default. This bead hardens that review model into the full reviewer-facing workflow.

The earlier task-lifecycle bead should already expose stable `needs_review` state and minimal review-export hooks. This bead builds on that contract to deliver the inspectable Markdown-plus-YAML workflow, structured triage import path, and operator-friendly correction loop that users actually work in.

## Technical Approach
1. Define the feedback artifact format keyed by stable task IDs and source IDs.
2. Support structured actions such as confirm, dismiss, wrong-class, missed-task, snooze, and merge duplicate, plus optional free-text comments.
3. Consume the stable review-lane contract from upstream task-lifecycle work so the full feedback workflow extends an existing task model rather than inventing a separate one.
4. Keep the artifact inspectable and manually editable without a custom frontend.
5. Connect imported feedback to tuning inputs, thresholds, and governance updates.
6. Preserve ideas and boundaries that would allow a later dedicated frontend without forcing one now.

## Success Criteria
- Structured triage actions round-trip cleanly through the feedback artifact.
- Reviewers can inspect task metadata, confidence, and source context while giving feedback.
- Feedback import can affect later heuristics or governance tuning inputs.
- The hardened feedback artifact is consistent with the earlier minimal review-export contract rather than diverging from it.
- Manual overrides persist across replay unless explicitly reset.

## Test Plan
- Add round-trip tests for confirmations, dismissals, merges, snoozes, wrong-class, and missed-task actions.
- Add parser tests for the YAML sidecar format and validation rules.
- Add integration tests proving imported feedback affects later classification or routing inputs deterministically.
- Add compatibility tests proving the hardened artifact can ingest the upstream review-lane contract without lossy translation.

## Considerations
- Structured actions should remain primary; free-text comments are supporting evidence, not the core protocol.
- Keep the format stable and human-readable to avoid introducing a frontend too early.
- This bead should harden the review workflow, not move core task-identity decisions out of the task-registry layer.

title: IM message reaction ingestion (full normative scope)
description:
## North star

Deliver **end-to-end IM emoji reaction capture** for the Lark-to-notes workflow so reactions are durable, replayable, auditable, and safely reflected in the vault and task-distillation pipeline—without contaminating the chat message ledger or overwriting human-authored notes.

## Why this epic exists

Reactions are lightweight social signals that often encode **acknowledgement, priority, assignment, or disagreement**. For a local “work intake” system they are high-signal when combined with message text, but **dangerous when over-interpreted**. This epic turns the normative requirements in `plan_for_lark_to_notes_workflow.md` (Message reaction ingestion section) into an implementable DAG.

## Architectural commitments (do not violate)

1. **Separate append-only store** for reaction events (not synthetic rows in `raw_messages`).
2. **Idempotent ingestion** keyed on **`header.event_id`** when present, otherwise the **documented deterministic surrogate** from the plan, with operator-visible warnings when surrogacy triggers.
3. **Append-only history**: deletes are first-class events; **effective reaction sets** are reproducible by ordered replay or an equivalent deterministic summary hash (tests must prove equivalence where both exist).
4. **Conservative distillation defaults**; any “emoji-only task” behavior is **opt-in** behind explicit operator configuration and must stamp **`policy_version`**.
5. **Machine-owned vault blocks** only; never rewrite prose outside those blocks.
6. **Governance and caps parity**: reaction streams obey the same **per-run / per-source caps**, **deferred backlog**, **`governance_version` explainability**, and **quarantine** contracts as other intake (plan: Ingestion Caps, Governance and Policy Versioning, Metadata to Preserve).
7. **Observable failure paths**: malformed envelopes, unknown emoji packs, partial backfills, missing OAuth scopes, and **capability blockers**—each must be countable, diagnosable, and **never silent-empty** when data is incomplete.
8. **Replay / reclassify / reconcile**: operators can repair **vault drift** and rerun downstream stages from local SQLite history without inventing alternate identities (plan: Replay, Reclassify, and Reconcile Contract).

## Dependency philosophy

Edges follow **dataflow and trust boundaries**: normative guardrails → permissions/scopes matrix → schema → ledger (+ orphan linkage) → capped intake → live/CLI/backfill → vault materialization → distillation (policy-aware) → replay/reconcile tooling → doctor/metrics (including effective version tuples) → comprehensive tests (unit, integration, property, failure-path, locking, redaction, e2e scripts with structured logs) → operator docs.

Backfill depends on live parsing **and** the permissions matrix so REST vs WS normalization and **missing-scope blockers** are handled consistently.

## AGENTS.md testing alignment (non-negotiable for closure)

This epic’s test beads intentionally mirror `AGENTS.md` **Testing Practice**: normalization/fingerprinting/heuristics/rendering **unit** coverage; **`lark-cli` adapter integration**; **replay and idempotency**; **golden-file** vault renders; **locking / single-writer** behavior around machine blocks; **failure-path** (malformed payloads, retries, quarantine, cap hits); **redaction / sensitive-source** handling for operator identity fields in `payload_json`. **E2E** work is explicitly **scripted** (not only pytest), with **NDJSON or JSON-lines structured logs**, stderr human narrative, documented exit codes, and correlation IDs suitable for post-mortem diffing in CI artifacts.

## Success criteria (epic level)

- Live worker persists `im.message.reaction.created_v1` / `deleted_v1` with the same durability standard as chat messages, under caps, with quarantine metrics.
- `sync-events` replays reaction envelopes with stable JSON counters; **preflight** surfaces missing scopes before silent no-op.
- Orphan reactions persist before parent messages arrive and **reconcile attach** without duplicate canonical identities.
- Vault raw notes show a **reaction timeline** per message inside explicit machine blocks; reconcile can repair drift from local ledger alone when sufficient.
- Distillation attaches `reaction_signal` evidence under **versioned rules** with `needs_review` for ambiguity; **policy_version** and **governance_version** appear on sampled rows in **doctor** output.
- Replay/reclassify/reconcile commands exist for operator-driven recovery with resumable diagnostics.
- CI runs **ruff**, **mypy --strict**, **pytest** reaction suites, and the **e2e logging harness** in a documented order.

## Out of scope (unless a child issue explicitly expands)

- Cross-tenant analytics, emoji sentiment ML, or automatic assignment of work to humans without review.
- Mutating Lark state (adding/removing reactions remotely) except via explicitly scoped future issues.

## Tooling note (agents)

The epic id `lw-pzj` is a **prefix** of all child ids; some `br` subcommands treat `lw-pzj` as ambiguous. Prefer `br show lw-pzj --no-db`, `br list --json`, or longer ids when mutating the epic itself.

title: Reaction ingestion: normative traceability and scope guardrails
description:
## Purpose

## Background

1. Unit tests — normalization, fingerprinting, heuristics, rendering primitives → map to `lw-pzj.10.5`, `lw-pzj.10.11`, distill/vault pure helpers.
2. Integration tests — `lark-cli` adapters / source normalization → `lw-pzj.10.6`, fixture pipes.
3. Replay and idempotency tests → `lw-pzj.10.2`, `lw-pzj.10.10`, `lw-pzj.13.1`.
4. Golden-file tests — raw / vault renders → `lw-pzj.10.4`, `lw-pzj.13.3`.
5. Locking and single-writer tests → `lw-pzj.10.8`.
6. Failure-path tests — malformed payloads, retries, quarantine, caps → `lw-pzj.10.10`, `lw-pzj.12.1`, `lw-pzj.12.2`, `lw-pzj.12.4`, and doctor contract `lw-pzj.9.1`.

title: Normative: privacy, retention, emoji semantics, and explicit non-goals
description:
## Goal

Document **privacy, retention, and semantics** decisions that affect storage and rendering.

## Topics

- Whether “who reacted” is considered sensitive in this deployment.
- Retention policy for deleted reactions vs created (tombstones vs hard delete).
- Emoji meaning: conservative interpretation guidelines; explicit false-positive examples.

## Output

Short ADR-style bullets suitable to paste into README (lw-pzj.11.*) without duplicating the full plan.

## Relation to lw-pzj.1.1

1.1 is structural mapping; 1.2 is policy/semantics that constrains schema columns and redaction.

title: Tests: fixtures, idempotency, CLI integration, golden vault renders
description:
## Purpose

Own the **entire verification surface** for reaction ingestion: fast unit loops, deterministic integration with `lark-cli` fixtures, property-based invariants for effective reaction sets, failure injection, concurrency controls on vault machine blocks, sensitive-source redaction, and **scripted e2e** runs with **structured logs** suitable for CI artifact capture.

## Alignment with AGENTS.md

This parent issue exists so implementation cannot “ship” without the categories enumerated in `AGENTS.md` under **Testing Practice** (normalization, adapters, replay/idempotency, golden renders, locking, failure paths, redaction) **plus** explicit **e2e operator scripts** and **logging contracts** demanded by this epic.

## How to close

Close only when **`lw-pzj.10.12` (CI gate)** completes: it aggregates every sibling test bead, enforces `uv run ruff check .`, `uv run ruff format --check .` (if configured), `uv run mypy --strict src tests`, `uv run pytest` for reaction suites, and the **e2e harness** smoke with logs retained under a documented artifact path.

## Dependency note

The parent **`lw-pzj.10` rollup** depends solely on **`lw-pzj.10.12`** so readiness flows through the sub-graph; individual subtasks carry fine-grained `blocks` edges to implementation issues (`lw-pzj.3`–`lw-pzj.15`, `lw-pzj.9.4`, etc.).

title: CI gate: ruff, mypy strict, pytest reaction suites, and e2e script smoke in documented order
description:
## Goal

1. `uv run ruff check .`
2. `uv run ruff format --check .` (when configured in repo)
3. `uv run mypy --strict src tests`
4. `uv run pytest` (include reaction suites by marker or path)
5. Run **`lw-pzj.10.7` e2e harness** with `ARTIFACT_DIR` set, upload/grep instructions for CI workers
6. Snapshot **`lw-pzj.9.4`** doctor fields for a golden “healthy/degraded/blocked” fixture set

## Logging

The gate script itself should log its argv, environment fingerprints (Python version, OS), and each step’s exit code + duration.

## Why a dedicated bead

Prevents “tests exist but CI doesn’t run them” drift—a recurring failure mode in polyglot repos.

title: Integration tests: lark-cli event fixture pipes, adapter boundaries, and structured per-stage JSON logs
description:
## Goal

## Logging contract (tests must assert)

Each processed batch emits at least one JSON line with: `ts`, `stage`, `event_type`, `event_id` (nullable), `source_id`, `message_id` (nullable), `result` (`accepted|quarantined|deferred|idempotent_skip`), `reason_code`, `duration_ms`, `run_id`.

## Why

Integration tests catch “works in pytest mocks” vs “works with real CLI framing” gaps early.

## References

title: E2E operator script: uv-driven harness replaying NDJSON with NDJSON+stderr logs and exit-code contract
description:
## Goal

Author a **scripted e2e harness** (shell + `uv run …` entrypoints acceptable) that runs a deterministic fixture through **`sync-events`-class paths**, optional **replay/reconcile** commands once `lw-pzj.13.*` lands, and collects **artifacts** for CI.

## Logging requirements (strict)

- **stdout**: NDJSON only (one JSON object per line) for machine parsing.
- **stderr**: human narrative with step banners, absolute timestamps, and explicit file paths for artifacts.
- Exit codes: document `0`, transient infra `retryable` codes vs hard failures (implementation defines integers but must be stable and documented in `lw-pzj.11.2`).

## CI behavior

Must fail loudly when reaction counters drift by **any** nonzero delta against golden expectations.

## Dependencies

Waits for **`lw-pzj.13`** so the harness can assert at least one replay/reconcile dry-run path when those commands exist (skip gates must be explicit in logs, not silent).

title: Single-writer tests: concurrent render attempts into machine-owned reaction blocks

title: Operator docs: README scope, limits, and troubleshooting for reactions
description:

title: Docs: operator runbook (sync-events, backfill, doctor triage)
description:
## Goal

## Include

Common failure strings and what they imply.

## Expanded procedures (post-bead graph)

1. **Preflight / permissions** (`lw-pzj.14.3`): interpret preflight failures vs doctor `blocked_missing_scope` vs `blocked_missing_api`.
2. **Caps / deferral triage** (`lw-pzj.9.1`): distinguish healthy empty streams from throttled intake; read `deferred_estimate` / cap-hit fields emitted by doctor JSON; schedule follow-up runs when deferrals are non-zero.
3. **Replay / reclassify / reconcile** (`lw-pzj.13.*`): when to run each mode; dry-run expectations; how to collect NDJSON artifacts from replay for support.
4. **CI gate reproduction** (`lw-pzj.10.12`): exact command sequence mirroring CI; where logs land (`ARTIFACT_DIR`); how to diff golden doctor snapshots.

## Logging guidance for operators

Document how to tee **stdout NDJSON** vs **stderr narrative** for `lw-pzj.10.7` harness runs, including redaction switches tied to `lw-pzj.1.2`.

title: Reactions: intake caps, deferred backlog, and governance/policy version stamping
description:
## Purpose

Implement **plan-faithful governance** for reaction streams: reactions are not a side-channel that bypasses caps, versioning, or explainability rules that apply to the rest of intake.

## Normative anchors

- **Ingestion Caps and Flood-Protection Contract** (global + per-source caps, explicit deferral, no silent truncation).
- **Governance and Policy Versioning for Live Explainability** (`governance_version`, `policy_version` attachment to durable rows and stage completions).
- **Message reaction ingestion** point 20: reactions obey the same caps, quarantine, governance_version, and policy_version explainability rules as other intake.

## Deliverables

- Durable representation of **deferred backlog** when caps hit (per source, per run, cursor-safe).
- Reaction ledger rows (and downstream stage completions that consume them) carry enough **version tuple** metadata for doctor sampling and replay audits.
- Retry behavior cannot bypass caps (storm protection).

## Why it blocks distill and doctor extensions

`lw-pzj.8` now depends on **`lw-pzj.12`** so reaction-derived confidence never ships without version-stamped explainability. `lw-pzj.9.4` also depends on **`lw-pzj.12`** to print believable effective versions on sampled rows.

title: Caps: per-run and per-source limits for reaction intake with explicit deferral rows
description:
## Goal

Encode **per-run** and **per-source** caps for reaction event intake, evaluated **before** expensive downstream stages (plan Ingestion Caps §1–§2).

## Deliverables

- Config surface (values + versioning hooks) consistent with the broader governance story.
- When caps hit: emit **explicit deferral rows** or backlog pointers keyed by `(run_id, source_id, cursor)` semantics used elsewhere—do not invent parallel cursor namespaces.

## Tests

`lw-pzj.10.10` must assert cap-hit behavior, deferred estimates, and retry convergence under the same cap policy.

title: Versions: stamp governance_version and policy_version on reaction rows and stage completions
description:
## Goal

Stamp **`governance_version`** and **`policy_version`** (or the repo’s equivalent tuple) onto **reaction ledger rows** and any **stage completion** records that consume reactions (plan Governance and Policy Versioning §1–§2, §6–§7).

## Why

Doctor and replay tooling must answer “which rules produced this row?” without guessing from timestamps alone.

## Cross-links

Feeds **`lw-pzj.9.4`** (doctor sampling) and **`lw-pzj.13.*`** (replay/reclassify invalidation semantics).

title: Diagnostics: cap-hit counts, deferred backlog estimates, and operator-visible truncation flags
description:
## Goal

Surface **cap-hit counts**, **deferred backlog estimates**, and **operator-visible truncation flags** in status/doctor JSON (plan Ingestion Caps §10).

## Logging

Structured logs should include: `run_id`, `source_id`, `cap_scope` (`global|per_source`), `cap_value`, `observed_count`, `deferred_estimate`, and `governance_version` in effect.

## Downstream

README/runbook (`lw-pzj.11.*`) references these fields verbatim so operators can grep artifacts.

title: Retry storms: verify retries respect the same cap policy as first-pass intake
description:
## Goal

Prove **retry storms** cannot bypass reaction caps (plan Ingestion Caps §6).

## Deliverables

- Deterministic tests that replay the same capped batch with retries and assert **no unbounded growth** in accepted rows beyond policy.
- Metrics distinguishing **benign duplicates** vs **retry amplification**.

## Relation to idempotency

Pairs with **`lw-pzj.3.3`** and **`lw-pzj.10.10`**; keep the story unified rather than splitting cap logic across SQLite and Python ad hoc.

title: Reactions: replay, reclassify, reconcile, and vault drift repair (local-first)
description:
## Purpose

Provide **operator-grade recovery modes** for reactions: rebuild derived artifacts, rerun classification under new rules, and repair vault drift **without fabricating identities**—matching the plan’s **Replay, Reclassify, and Reconcile Contract**.

## Normative anchors

- Replay rebuilds derived stages from immutable history + ledger/checkpoints **without mutating raw** rows for already-captured revisions.
- Reclassify reruns only stages whose semantics depend on rules or `policy_version` changes.
- Reconcile explains repairs in terms of **existing provenance**; if uncertainty remains, **quarantine** rather than silent note mutation.
- Reaction-specific **vault drift** repair path when local SQLite suffices (plan Message reaction ingestion §8).

## Deliverables

- Scoped commands (exact CLI shape left to implementation bead) with **dry-run**, **since/until**, and **source filters** where applicable.
- Durable **partial-run diagnostics** so the next run resumes safely (plan replay contract §7).

## Dependencies

Requires **ledger + vault + distill** surfaces (`lw-pzj.3`, `lw-pzj.7`, `lw-pzj.8`) **and** governance/caps stamping (`lw-pzj.12`) so replay/reclassify/reconcile semantics are meaningful rather than cosmetic.

title: Replay: scoped reaction replay rebuilds derived stages without mutating immutable raw rows
description:
## Goal

Implement **scoped replay** for reaction-derived stages: rebuild derived outputs from immutable reaction history + ledger/checkpoints **without mutating immutable raw rows** for already captured revisions (plan Replay §1–§2).

## Logging contract

Replay runs emit **NDJSON progress lines**: `stage`, `source_id`, `message_id` (nullable), `rows_processed`, `rows_skipped_idempotent`, `cursor_high_water`, `governance_version`, `policy_version`, `duration_ms`.

## Tests

`lw-pzj.10.7` uses this logging format as part of the e2e harness assertions.

title: Reclassify: rerun distill-only stages under bumped rules or policy_version without raw rewrite
description:
## Goal

Implement **reclassify** for reaction-aware distill outputs: rerun only stages whose behavior depends on rules or `policy_version` changes, without rewriting raw reaction rows (plan Replay §3).

## Deliverables

- Explicit invalidation map: which stages rerun when `policy_version` bumps vs when only `governance_version` changes.
- Deterministic outputs captured in golden tests where stable.

## Safety

Never silently rewrite historic classifications; record supersession explicitly per existing task lifecycle patterns.

title: Reconcile: repair vault reaction blocks vs ledger using local SQLite when sufficient
description:
## Goal

Implement **reconcile** for **vault reaction blocks** vs SQLite ledger when local data suffices—**no re-fetch** path when upstream is unnecessary (plan Message reaction ingestion §8 + Reconcile §4–§5).

## Deliverables

- Drift detectors: missing machine block, stale summary hash vs ordered replay, stage completion gaps.
- Repair resumes from earliest invalid stage with provenance echoed into logs.

## Tests

Golden markdown in **`lw-pzj.10.4`** plus e2e harness checks in **`lw-pzj.10.7`**.

title: Failure resume: durable partial-run diagnostics so the next operator run can continue safely

title: Reactions: permissions, scopes, and doctor-visible capability blockers
description:
## Purpose

Make **missing permissions** a first-class, operator-visible **blocker** rather than an empty success: reactions are useless without the correct IM read scopes, and tenants differ on bot-in-chat constraints.

## Normative anchors

- Message reaction ingestion §9: operators configure scopes such as **`im:message.reactions:read`** (and related IM read scopes); missing scope is a **blocker**, not soft-empty success.
- Doctor/README must report **capability blockers** when APIs are unavailable (plan §5, §9).

## Deliverables

- A maintained **scope matrix** (human doc + machine-checkable list where feasible).
- **Doctor** output distinguishes “no reactions observed” vs “cannot observe due to scope/API”.
- **Preflight** checks in CLI/worker startup paths so silent no-op runs are rare.

## Dependency rationale

Depends on **`lw-pzj.1`** so privacy/redaction policy (who reacted is sensitive) informs which scopes are mandatory vs optional for a deployment.

title: Scope matrix: document required IM scopes including im:message.reactions:read and bot constraints
description:
## Goal

Author the **scope matrix** for reaction telemetry: required vs optional Lark OAuth scopes, bot-in-chat caveats, and tenant-specific constraints (Message reaction ingestion §9).

## Output artifacts

## Non-goals

This bead does not implement token acquisition—only documents and structures requirements for implementers.

title: Doctor blockers: explicit capability-blocker status when scopes or APIs are missing
description:
## Goal

Extend **doctor JSON** to classify reaction ingestion health into explicit states: `healthy`, `degraded_partial_history`, `blocked_missing_scope`, `blocked_missing_api`, `quarantine_elevated` (exact enum left to implementation, but must be **mutually exclusive** and **stable** for CI).

## Why

“Zero reactions” is ambiguous: it could be healthy or a misconfiguration. Operators deserve deterministic triage.

## Tests

Asserted via **`lw-pzj.10.12`** doctor snapshot fixtures.

title: Preflight: startup or CLI dry-run checks surface permission gaps before silent no-op runs
description:
## Goal

Add **preflight** checks to worker/CLI startup: validate scopes and capability prerequisites, print actionable remediation, and exit non-zero in strict modes when reactions are enabled but unusable.

## Logging

Preflight logs MUST include: `check_name`, `result`, `remediation_hint`, `tenant_app_id` (if safe), and correlation `run_id`.

## Dependencies conceptually

Consumes **`lw-pzj.14.1`** outputs; blocks “silent live runs” that waste hours debugging.

title: Reactions: orphan backlog, message arrival attachment, and linkage invariants
description:
## Purpose

Implement the plan’s **linkage** rule: accept reaction events **before** the parent chat message exists in `raw_messages`, queue them safely, and reconcile attachment when the message arrives—**without minting duplicate canonical identities**.

## Normative anchors

- Message reaction ingestion §4: backlog + reconcile after message arrives.
- Metadata §17–§18: preserve identifiers needed for deterministic **effective reaction set** reconstruction.

## Deliverables

- Explicit **orphan state machine** in the ledger layer (not ad hoc flags scattered through renderers).
- Reconciliation path that updates correlation fields and clears orphan backlog with **metrics** (depth, age, attach latency) surfaced to **doctor** and asserted in tests (`lw-pzj.10.11`, `lw-pzj.15.3`).

## Downstream

`lw-pzj.7` depends on **`lw-pzj.15`** so vault materialization never pretends parent messages always precede reactions.

title: Orphan persistence: accept reactions before parent message lands; durable orphan queue state
description:
## Goal

Persist reaction events when the parent chat message row is **absent**, using an explicit **orphan queue** model (state + timestamps + last_seen) rather than implicit NULL foreign keys scattered across modules.

## Invariants

- Never mint a second canonical `message_id` identity when the message eventually arrives.
- Preserve full `payload_json` for replay per plan Metadata §17.

## Tests

Covered by **`lw-pzj.10.11`** (property/effective-set interplay) and failure fixtures in **`lw-pzj.10.1`**.

title: Attachment: reconcile link to captured messages without minting duplicate canonical identities
description:
## Goal

On message ingestion, **attach** queued orphan reactions to the canonical message record, updating correlation fields and clearing orphan state with a single transactional narrative.

## Reconciliation logging

Emit structured logs: `orphan_batch_id`, `message_id`, `attached_count`, `still_orphan` (should trend to zero), `elapsed_ms`.

## Coordination

Must align with chat intake ledger semantics so observation counters remain coherent (shared intake ledger contract in plan).

title: Metrics: orphan depth, age, and attach latency surfaced to doctor and test assertions
description:
## Goal

Expose **orphan backlog depth**, **age histogram buckets**, and **attach latency** to doctor JSON and test assertions so operators can detect stuck attachment paths early.

## Cross-links

Feeds **`lw-pzj.9.*`** dashboards and **`lw-pzj.10.11`** expectations for deterministic orphan drains.

title: Storage: message_reaction_events table, indexes, and migration wiring
description:
## Purpose

Add a **versioned SQLite home** for reaction events that supports append-only ingestion, idempotent replays, and forward-compatible migrations.

## Background

Chat history already lives in structured tables with a migration story (`storage/schema.py`, `storage/db.py`). Reactions must not overload `raw_messages` because they are not messages; different cardinality, different dedupe keys, and different render semantics.

## Design constraints

- Stable **dedupe identity** compatible with Lark event IDs when present.
- Indexes that make **chat_id + message_id + time** queries cheap for vault materialization.
- Migration must be safe on existing user DBs (empty table, no data loss).

## Acceptance sketch

- Fresh init creates the table; existing DB migrates deterministically.
- `PRAGMA user_version` / project schema semver reflects the bump (follow local conventions).

## Dependencies

Blocked until normative traceability (lw-pzj.1) closes so column choices align with policy.
close_reason: Storage epic: v9 message_reaction_events + migration + doctor surface hooks complete.

title: Schema: DDL columns, Lark field mapping, idempotency indexes, and dedupe semantics
description:
## Goal

Define the **canonical column set** for `message_reaction_events` and map each to Lark payload fields.

## Include

- Tenant/chat/message identifiers, reaction type, emoji key, user id fields, action (create/delete), raw envelope ref (hash or blob pointer if used), ingest timestamps.

## Avoid

Storing huge redundant JSON if a hash + quarantine file is the local policy—decide explicitly.

## Handoff

Feeds migration SQL and ORM/dataclass models in lw-pzj.3.1.

## Merged from closed duplicates

### Former `lw-pzj.2.2`: Schema: idempotency indexes, dedupe keys, and replay semantics

## Goal

Specify **indexes and uniqueness** for idempotent ingestion and common queries.

## Must handle

- Duplicate delivery (WS + replay + backfill) without row explosion.

title: Intake: reaction event model, append-only ledger, and idempotent persistence
description:
## Purpose

Implement the **authoritative internal representation** for reaction events and the writer API used by live WS, CLI replay, and (later) backfill.

## Background

The live path today funnels `im.message.receive_v1` into chat intake (`live/chat_events.py` → ledger → `raw_messages`). Reactions need parallel **intake** with shared operational metrics but **separate persistence**.

## Non-negotiables

- Append-only insert semantics; updates only for derived bookkeeping if explicitly justified.
- Idempotent inserts under replay; crash-safe transaction boundaries.
- Correlation: must be able to relate a reaction event to a captured message row where possible, without pretending the reaction is a message.

## Suggested module boundaries

- Typed parse/normalize layer (pure)
- DB writer (SQLite)
- Metrics hook surface (counts, last error)

## Downstream consumers

Live (lw-pzj.4), CLI (lw-pzj.5), backfill (lw-pzj.6), vault (lw-pzj.7), doctor (lw-pzj.9), tests (lw-pzj.10) all assume this ledger exists and is trustworthy.

title: Distillation: reaction_signal evidence and conservative promotion heuristics
description:
## Purpose

Convert reaction history into **task evidence** (`reaction_signal`) under versioned, testable heuristics.

## Background

This is the highest-risk layer: reactions are suggestive, not declarative. The normative plan calls for **conservative defaults** and explicit review lanes.

## Heuristic design requirements

- Rules are versioned; bumping rules must not silently rewrite historic meaning without an explicit migration story.
- Emoji-only promotion must be **opt-in** behind config gates.
- Ambiguous patterns route to `needs_review` with human-readable reasons.

## Governance coupling (do not skip)

`lw-pzj.8` is **blocked by `lw-pzj.12`** so reaction-derived routing never ships without **cap/deferral explainability** and **`policy_version` / `governance_version` stamping** on the underlying rows and stage completions. Distill heuristics must emit diagnostics that cite those versions when confidence shifts.

## Interop

Consumes ledger rows (`lw-pzj.3`), orphan/linkage semantics (`lw-pzj.15`), and human-visible vault context (`lw-pzj.7`) so mismatches between rendered and distilled views surface as `needs_review` with explicit codes.

## Testing note

Golden expectations should live beside distill fixtures; property and failure suites are owned under `lw-pzj.10.*` and **`lw-pzj.10.10`**.

title: Observability: doctor JSON metrics, quarantine hooks, reaction health
description:
## Purpose

Expose reaction pipeline health in **`doctor --json`** (and related ops surfaces) so failures are obvious before notes drift silently.

## Background

Silent failure modes: WS connected but parser dropping unknown events, backfill stalled, vault writer blocked on permissions, **orphan backlog** growing, or **cap deferrals** hiding incomplete history.

## Deliverables

- Counters: events ingested, quarantined, rendered, distilled, **orphan depth/age**, **cap hits**, **deferred estimates** (see consolidated contract in **`lw-pzj.9.1`** including capability-blocker enums).
- Last error metadata (sanitized): type, code, correlation id.
- **Capability blockers** surfaced distinctly from healthy empties (implemented under **`lw-pzj.9.1`**, not a parallel bead).

## Extensions

`lw-pzj.9.4` adds **sampled effective `governance_version` / `policy_version` tuples** so operators can correlate surprising distill or render changes with configuration drift.

## Dependencies

Ledger (`lw-pzj.3`) and permissions/capability classification (`lw-pzj.14`) must exist so metrics are meaningful rather than cosmetic.

title: Comprehensive testing: full unit coverage + E2E integration scripts with structured logging
description:
## Goal

Close every meaningful gap in the unit test suite and build complete end-to-end integration scripts that emit structured, detailed log output. The result should be a test suite where:

- Every public function and every code branch is exercised by a real-code test (SQLite in memory, real temp files, real Python objects -- no test doubles that bypass implementation)
- Three places currently use monkeypatch (render IOError, lock acquire fail, pipeline FAILED propagation) -- replace the avoidable ones with real-fs equivalents; keep only the lock-acquire case which genuinely cannot be triggered without patching
- execute_daemon in runtime/executor.py has zero behavioral tests -- fix that
- feedback/store.py get_event and has_manual_override have zero direct tests -- fix that
- logging.py configure_logging is completely untested -- fix that
- distill/routing.py missing case: escalate_to_llm=False with provider present (fast-path skip) -- add it
- CLI has no tests for error paths: bad YAML feedback import, unknown task_id feedback import, render with failed tasks, doctor with empty DB -- add them
- No E2E scripts exist under scripts/ at all -- build them with rich structlog JSON output
- No integration test that validates the structured log events emitted by the pipeline -- build one

## Acceptance

All unit gap beads done => 464 => 520+ tests, all pass.
E2E script beads done => scripts/integration_run.py works standalone.
Integration logging tests done => stderr NDJSON validates log events.

## Sub-beads
lw-tst.1: feedback/store.py gaps
lw-tst.2: feedback/service.py gaps
lw-tst.3: logging.py configure_logging
lw-tst.4: distill/routing.py missing path

lw-tst.7: Real-fs render failure tests (replace monkeypatch)

lw-tst.9: Full operator CLI workflow E2E
lw-tst.10: scripts/integration_run.py standalone script
lw-tst.11: Multi-source isolation E2E
lw-tst.12: Performance smoke tests

title: Integration tests: capture structlog NDJSON stderr and validate specific log events from full pipeline
description:
Create tests/test_integration_logging.py.

PURPOSE: Prove the pipeline emits the structured log events it promises, with correct fields. This is the contract between implementation and any monitoring layer.

IMPLEMENTATION PATTERN:
def _capture_logs(fn, *, level='DEBUG'):
    buf = io.StringIO()
    old_err = sys.stderr
    sys.stderr = buf
    configure_logging(level, json_logs=True)
    try: fn()
    finally:
        sys.stderr = old_err
        configure_logging('WARNING')  # restore
    lines = [l for l in buf.getvalue().splitlines() if l.strip()]
    return [json.loads(l) for l in lines]

title: Design a compatibility layer for current worker JSON config without keeping worker semantics as authoritative
description:
Background
- The old worker uses a JSON config with concrete operational value, but the project wants the in-repo source model to be authoritative.
- A compatibility layer is the safest way to preserve operator continuity without preserving worker-specific semantics forever.

Goal
- Define how current worker-style config is read, validated, translated, and eventually migrated into the canonical in-repo model.

Scope
- Input compatibility with the existing JSON shape.
- Mapping to the in-repo watched-source model.
- Validation and error semantics when unsupported combinations appear.
acceptance_criteria:
- There is a documented mapping from current worker JSON keys to the in-repo source and runtime model.
- Unsupported or lossy mappings are explicitly called out instead of being hidden.
- The compatibility layer is framed as migration support, not as a second configuration authority.
close_reason: Merged into canonical feature lw-m5k.2 during backlog consolidation

title: Design the normalized revision-aware source model for documents, comments, and replies
description:
Background
- Document content, comments, and replies are mutable and revision-bearing, which makes them fundamentally different from the message-centric chat model already in the repo.
- If we skip the modeling step, any later document adapter will either lose important semantics or fake them unsafely.

Goal
- Define the canonical normalized record contract for document-side sources before adapter implementation claims parity.

Scope
- Record type distinctions.
- Stable external IDs and parent anchors.
- Revision, lifecycle, canonical link, participant, and provenance fields.
acceptance_criteria:
- The normalized model distinguishes document content, comments, and replies without collapsing them into a single chat-style record.
- The model explains how revisions, deletes, parent anchors, canonical links, and provenance are represented.
- The result is detailed enough to drive schema work and adapter normalization without re-opening the modeling question.
close_reason: Merged into canonical feature lw-m5k.4 during backlog consolidation

title: Normalize document surfaces into the canonical contract and define incremental sync plus backfill behavior
description:
Background
- Transport alone is not enough. The project only gains real document coverage when those payloads are normalized into the canonical revision-aware contract and integrated into backfill plus incremental sync.
- This is the task where document-side live ingestion becomes operational rather than merely discoverable.

Goal
- Define and implement the normalization and sync semantics that let document surfaces feed the canonical pipeline safely.

Scope
- Normalization into the revision-aware contract.
- Incremental sync semantics.
- Backfill behavior.
- Boundaries between polling, replay, and later reclassification.
acceptance_criteria:
- Document content, comments, and replies have a clear normalized path into the canonical store.
- Backfill and incremental sync behavior for document surfaces are explicit and replay-safe.
- The design avoids pretending that mutable document surfaces can be treated like simple append-only chat messages.
close_reason: Merged into canonical feature lw-m5k.5 during backlog consolidation
